title: Sentinel Shield consumer-validation record
description: One record emitted by a real-consumer validation driver (e.g. tests/prod/201-node-consumers.sh) for a single check against a genuine downstream consumer project. Records are line-delimited JSON (one object per line). A record captures WHICH consumer and package manager was exercised, WHICH gate/check ran, its status, a STABLE reason_code, and whether the check was proven LIVE (real toolchain executed) or STRUCTURAL (logic/contract asserted without executing the toolchain). status=skip is NEVER a pass and MUST carry a reason.

schema_version.description: Consumer-validation record schema version. Fixed at '1'.

consumer.description: Consumer fixture the check ran against (e.g. node-service, react-app, pm-variants/npm).

package_manager.description: Authoritative package manager for the consumer, or 'none' when the check is manager-agnostic.

check.description: Stable identifier of the specific check (e.g. pm-authority, lockfile-authority, one-of-test-runner, typecheck-mutation, rollback-byte-for-byte).

gate.description: The engine gate the check exercises.

status.description: Outcome. 'pass' = assertion held; 'fail' = a real defect the gate correctly caught OR a validation failure; 'skip' = could not run (toolchain/network absent) — recorded as a limitation, never counted as a pass.

reason_code.description: Stable machine code for the outcome (e.g. OK, MULTIPLE_AUTHORITATIVE_LOCKFILES, MANAGER_MISMATCH, MISSING_LOCKFILE, INVALID_MANAGER, TS_COMPILE_FAIL, ESLINT_ERROR, TEST_FAIL, LOCKFILE_RESTORED, LIVE_UNAVAILABLE).

mode.description: 'live' = the real toolchain/manager was executed and observed; 'structural' = logic, contract, or fixture shape was asserted without executing the downstream toolchain.

detail.description: Optional human-readable detail (paths, commands, observed values).

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 UTC time the record was emitted.
